title: Battle of Khuiumin

{{Quote|The Imperial Star Destroyers ''Bombard'' and ''Crusader'' limped back to Ord Grovner Naval Base this week after dealing a death-blow to the arrogant and once-almighty Eyttyrmin Pirates of the Khuiumin system.|[[TriNebulon News/Legends|TriNebulon News]]|<ref name="GNN6" />}}
The '''Battle of Khuiumin''' was a suppression operation<ref name="PP">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref> conducted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] onto the [[Eyttyrmin Batiiv]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], resulting in the pirates' [[Death/Legends|destruction]]. The survivors formed the [[Khuiumin Survivors]] and vowed revenge against the Imperial commanders of that battle.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|The two destroyers were dispatched by Admiral Freeda last week to destroy the pirate menace. The murderous rogues chose to stand and fight rather than abandon their base, confident in their ability to hold the system.|TriNebulon News|<ref name="GNN6" />}}
For [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the infamous [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] had been using the [[Khuiumin system/Legends|Khuiumin system]] as a base of operations. The [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]] finally dispatched the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] ''[[Bombard]]'', commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Alistar Dadefra]], and [[Crusader (Victory-class)|''Crusader'']],<ref name="AtPA" /> commanded by [[Zlece Oonaar]],<ref name="IJ" /> to put an end to their threat. Although the Eyttyrmin Batiivs were aware of the impending Imperial attack, they were confident in their ability to defeat a pair of "antiquated" ''Victory''-class [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. Instead of fleeing, the pirates chose to gather their forces and make their stand at [[Courkrus|Khuiumin]].<ref name="AtPA" />

The pirate [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] numbered over 140 ships, including about 70 [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], 50 [[Missile boat/Legends|missile boats]] converted from [[Yacht/Legends|yachts]] and other civilian ships, several dozen [[Freighter/Legends|cargo vessels]] armed with with improvised weaponry, and 28 stolen [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] and [[Patrol ship/Legends|patrol ships]].<ref name="AtPA" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|Though the pirate armada, consisting of over 150 ships, boasted ample firepower to pose a serious threat to two Victory-class destroyers, superior Imperial tactics and training carried the day in the end. The three-day battle ranged across the entire Khuiumin system, and culminated in the destruction of the pirate base itself.|TriNebulon News|<ref name="GNN6" />}}
''Bombard'' and ''Crusader'' exited [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and launched their [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]]. While ''Crusader'' offered supporting fire from a distance, Captain Dadefra chose to plunge his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] into the middle of the pirate fleet. Although it was screened by TIE fighters, the ''Bombard'' took heavy damage. However, through a bold move by the Imperials, the tide of the battle began to turn in their favor.<ref name="AtPA" />

Using its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|projectors]], ''Bombard'' grabbed hold of several corvettes. [[Turbolaser/Legends|Turbolaser]] fire stripped the corvettes of most of their weaponry, and the helpless ships were reeled in to shield the Star Destroyer from the other pirate ships. Captain Dadefra ordered his ship to jam communications, sowing confusion amongst the pirate fleet. The Eyttyrmin Batiivs were caught in a dilemma: they weren't able to get a clear shot at the Star Destroyer, but they were also unwilling to destroy their own heaviest ships. The pirates made repeated, uncoordinated attempts to destroy ''Bombard''{{'s}} tractor beam projectors, but they were beaten back with precise turbolaser fire and volleys of [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]].<ref name="AtPA" />

While the pirates continued to make futile attacks upon his ship, Captain Dadefra deployed his [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|Zero-G Assault Stormtrooper]]s. Braving enemy fire, the troopers flew across space and seized control of the nearby corvettes. Dadefra then sent half of ''Bombard''{{'s}} gunners to his newly acquired ships. Suddenly faced with not only Star Destroyers, but their own corvettes, the Eyttyrmin Batiiv forces panicked and flew into disarray. Most of the pirates tried to flee the [[Star system/Legends|system]] by escaping into hyperspace. However, they were easily intercepted by ''Crusader'', which had remained at a distance for this exact purpose. Another group of pirates fled to their [[Eyttyrmin Batiiv stronghold|base]] on Khuiumin's surface, where they prepared their defenses for one final stand. The Eyttyrmin Batiivs who chose to continue fighting were annihilated.<ref name="AtPA" /> The quick thinking of a young pirate named [[Jacob Nive]] saved many of the remaining Eyttyrmin Batiiv from certain [[Death/Legends|death]], but could not change the battle's outcome.

Less than a [[Standard day/Legends|day]] after the battle, ''Crusader'' delivered the final blow to the base on Khuiumin. A barrage of [[assault concussion missile]]s [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|shattered]] the base's [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and destroyed the remaining pirates. The Eyttyrmin Batiivs were completely routed. In a single battle, their once sizable fleet was wiped away. Out of 8,000 pirates, fewer than 275 escaped with their lives. Imperial casualties numbered only 86 dead and 238 wounded.<ref name="AtPA" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|...Fewer than 275 out of 8,000 escaped alive.|Account of the Imperial defeat of the Eyttyrmin Batiiv pirate gang|<ref name="GG9" />}}
The few surviving pirates took with them a single corvette, the ''[[Backstab]]'', whose Captain Jacob Nive led them as the [[Khuiumin Survivors]]. The survivors eventually learned the names of the Imperial commanders and vowed revenge. Jacob Nive organized the group into a high-skilled mercenary-like group<ref name="GG9" /> and even integrated other, smaller pirate bands into his organization. Revenge finally came first in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], when the Khuiumin Survivors aided the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in the [[Battle of Derilyn]] and destroyed the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Brazen]]'', Alistar Dadefra's new command<ref name="OE">[[Operation: Elrood (book)|''Operation: Elrood'']]</ref> and [[Attack on the Galaxy Chance|tracked down]] and killed Zlece Oonaar.<ref name="IJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Khuiumin first appeared in the short story ''[[Against the Pirate Armada]]'', published in [[West End Games]]' ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'' in [[1987]]. When the game was updated to the Second Edition, the story was republished in the [[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|revised ''Sourcebook'']]. The battle has since been mentioned in several works that included the Khuiumin Survivors.

The roleplaying magazine [[Avalon 1|''Avalon'' 1]], which was released without [[Lucas Licensing]] approval and thus is considered non-canon, introduced the pirate [[Omin]] and stated that some survivors of the Battle of Khuiumin fled under their leadership. They went to the [[Tragan system]], in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], and founded [[Providence (lair)|Providence]], with the remains of their now unusable starships.<ref name="AVAL">{{Avalonmag|1|Providence (Avalon)}}</ref>
